metadata.title: Enhance markdown TODO parser + native importer/exporter pipeline
metadata.description: Add registerImporter/registerExporter('todos') native pipeline; improve parser: nested sub-tasks, multi-file --glob, ## section->tags, (p1)/! priority; flags --section, --closed-as, --glob
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Shipped: native todos importer/exporter pipeline + enhanced parser (nested sub-tasks, --glob multi-file, ## section->tags, (p1)/! priority) + --section/--closed-as/--glob/--no-section-tags flags. All verification passed (build, tsc --noEmit, test, functional round-trip).

metadata.title: Idempotent --upsert import (no duplicates on re-import)
metadata.description: Add an opt-in --upsert flag to 'pm todos import' that updates existing pm items instead of creating duplicates on re-import. Matches each incoming markdown checkbox to an existing item by (1) the embedded <!-- pm-id --> provenance comment the exporter emits, then (2) a stable title signature for hand-written files. Also parses the <!-- pm-id --> comment out of the title on every import so export->edit->import-with-upsert round-trips land edits on the same items. Default (no flag) behavior is unchanged: items are always created.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.acceptance_criteria: Default import still duplicates on re-run; --upsert keeps the item count stable and updates in place; export->import --upsert preserves ids and creates no duplicates; status not re-sent when unchanged; non-recursive bounded spawning; unit tests + functional test pass; changelog:check exits 0.

metadata.title: preflight: fail-fast todo.txt/markdown syntax gate before import
metadata.description: Add a preflight syntax validation gate that validates every input file (reusing validateTodoFile) BEFORE pm todos import writes anything to the pm store. Malformed input (bad due date, out-of-range priority marker) aborts immediately with a clear, actionable error naming file/line/problem and a non-zero exit; no items are created. Also register the SDK registerPreflight override (scoped to the import command) so the manifest 'preflight' capability is genuinely exercised, and make the manifest accurate.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.resolution: Added preflightValidateImportFiles() invoked at the start of all import entry points (todos import command, todos importer, legacy todos-import) before any pm-store write; reuses validateTodoFile. Registered scoped registerPreflight override. Manifest: kept 'schema' (required for command flags), added 'preflight', so capabilities are now truthful. Added 5 unit tests + README preflight note.
metadata.expected_result: Malformed todo.txt/markdown import aborts immediately with a clear error and creates no items; valid input imports normally; export and validate are not blocked.
metadata.actual_result: Confirmed via functional test on a real scratch pm: malformed todo.txt and markdown each abort with exit 1 and 'count: 0'; --dry-run also gated; valid todo.txt (3) and markdown (2) import correctly; export exit 0; validate unaffected. 42/42 unit tests pass; build + tsc clean.

metadata.title: Hands-on functional test pass 2026-05-29 (real data)
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Both bugs fixed, rebuilt, re-tested green. pm-todos functional pass complete.

metadata.title: Deepen pm-todos: todo.txt + GH task-list + validate + grouping + full field round-trip
metadata.description: Add todo.txt import/export, GitHub task-list export, todos validate command, --group-by sectioning, and full field round-trip (type/priority/tags/deadline/status/assignee) in markdown. Backward compatible: default markdown byte-identical.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: All enhancements shipped: todo.txt import/export (--format todotxt), GitHub task-list export (--format tasklist), todos validate command, --group-by status|sprint|type. Default markdown kept byte-identical (verified). 26 unit tests green, typecheck clean, functionally verified end-to-end with real data incl. lossless todo.txt round-trip. Capabilities kept at [commands,schema,importers] - empirically schema is REQUIRED for command activation in this extension shape (dropping it causes Unknown command todos).

metadata.title: todos import/export return error object instead of throwing (exit 0 on failure)
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: throw in import (no-arg/read-fail) and export (list-all fail). Verified exit 1 for missing-file/no-arg; export-stdout exit 0.

metadata.title: Command handlers threw plain Error (no exitCode) → runtime double-invocation
metadata.description: todos import/export threw plain Error without numeric exitCode; pm re-invokes the handler on the unhandled path. Added CommandError mirroring SDK EXIT_CODE; missing-arg=2, missing-file=3, other=1; single invocation.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Fixed: CommandError carries exitCode. Verified round-trip import/export + error paths exit 2/3 single invocation.

metadata.title: todos import --dry-run silently ignored (still writes)
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Fixed via readBoolOption. Verified --dry-run writes nothing (before=0 after=0).

metadata.title: todo.txt format import + export (--format todotxt)
metadata.description: Parse/serialize todo.txt: x done marker, (A)-(Z) priority, +project/@context tags, due:YYYY-MM-DD. Map priority letters<->pm numeric, projects/contexts<->tags, due<->deadline, completion<->status.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Shipped todo.txt import+export via --format todotxt. parseTodoTxt/serializeTodoTxt handle x marker, (A-Z) priority, +project/@context->tags, due:->deadline, completion->status. Functionally verified: imported hand-written todo.txt, fields mapped correctly (prio/tags/due/status), lossless round-trip re-export.

metadata.title: Release readiness hardening for pm-todos
metadata.description: Prepare pm-todos as a production-ready pm package with package-local pm tracking, generated changelog data, CI and daily release automation.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.acceptance_criteria: Package installs with pm install; npm release gate passes; changelog is generated from package-local pm data; tracked files and reachable git history have no secret pattern hits.

metadata.title: Decision: priority-letter<->number mapping
metadata.description: todo.txt (A)=highest, pm 0=highest. Map (A)->0,(B)->1,(C)->2,(D)->3,(E)->4; F-Z->4. pm N->letter chr(65+N), clamp to A-E range for export (N>4 unusual).
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Decision recorded: (A)->0,(B)->1,(C)->2,(D)->3,(E)->4; F-Z clamp to 4 (lowest). Export pm N->chr(65+N) clamped to A-E. Implemented in priorityLetterToPm/pmPriorityToLetter with unit tests; functionally verified (A->prio0, C->prio2 on real import).

metadata.title: GitHub-flavored task-list export (--format tasklist)
metadata.description: Export - [ ]/- [x] task lists with --group-by status|sprint|type section headers.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Shipped GitHub task-list export via --format tasklist with --group-by status|sprint|type. renderTaskList emits - [ ]/- [x] under ## headings with <!-- id --> round-trip comments. Verified group-by sprint output.

metadata.title: Full field round-trip in markdown (type/priority/tags/deadline/status/assignee)
metadata.description: Embed and parse metadata so fields survive markdown round-trip.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Delivered full field round-trip (priority/tags/deadline/status) via the new todotxt format, which is genuinely lossless (verified: (A)->prio0, +project->tags, due:->deadline, x->closed survive import->re-export). Deliberately did NOT embed extra metadata into DEFAULT markdown checkboxes: the hard constraint requires default markdown export to stay byte-identical, and registerItemFields custom fields have no pm create/update setter (SDK issue #97), so markdown stays the lightweight title+type+id format. assignee/sprint surface in export via --group-by sprint/type rather than inline. Confirmed pm create supports --deadline/--assignee/--sprint and list-all --json returns them.

metadata.title: Production-readiness audit 2026-05-28
metadata.author: codex

metadata.title: Add publish retry + provenance fallback to release workflow
metadata.description: Replace the npm publish step in .github/workflows/release.yml with a resilient version that (1) skips if the version is already published, (2) retries up to 3 times with 30s sleep, and (3) falls back to publishing without --provenance after persistent registry 404s. Permanent fix for the 2026-05-27 npm E404 outage that left all 19 packages tagged in git but unpublished on npm.
metadata.author: codex

metadata.title: --group-by status|sprint|type sectioning on export
metadata.description: Group exported items into markdown sections.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Shipped --group-by status|sprint|type for markdown and tasklist export. groupItems buckets by field with (unassigned) sorted last. Default markdown (no group-by or group-by status) kept byte-identical (renderDefaultMarkdown unit-tested against exact historical layout).

metadata.title: todos validate <file> command
metadata.description: Parse + report issues (unparseable lines, unknown priorities, bad dates) without importing; nonzero exit on structural problems; --json.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Shipped todos validate command. validateTodoFile reports errors (bad due date via real calendar check, out-of-range (pN) markers) and warnings (empty text, unparseable checkbox) without importing. Nonzero exit on structural errors (verified single-invocation, exit 1). --json returns full issues array on stdout for valid files; stderr summary always. 7 unit tests.

metadata.title: todo.txt date + key:value round-trip fidelity, --status import, export --sort
metadata.description: Fix todo.txt serializer dropping parsed creation/completion dates and key:value tokens (now lossless round-trip). Add --status to set status for open items on import (complements --closed-as). Add --sort priority|deadline|title to export. All additive; default markdown export byte-identical.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Shipped: date+kv round-trip fix, --status import, --sort export; tests green, byte-identical default markdown verified

metadata.title: ci: fix release workflow step ordering
metadata.description: Swap changelog regeneration before release:check so changelog:check sees the version-bumped CHANGELOG.md instead of failing the gate.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Swapped changelog generation before release checks so changelog:check sees updated file. Verified workflow dispatch then triggers npm publish + GH release.

metadata.title: Fix .gitignore + release workflow conflict for dist/
metadata.description: Workflow's 'git add dist' failed in CI because dist/ was in .gitignore. Removed dist/ from .gitignore so workflow can stage built output. Manually published pm-todos@2026.5.26 to npm to align with the other 16 pm-* packages released today.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Removed dist/ from .gitignore. Published pm-todos@2026.5.26. Future daily runs will succeed at the Commit release files step.

metadata.title: Add bun-install verification to release workflow
metadata.description: Smoke-test that the just-published npm package installs cleanly via bun via daily release workflow.
metadata.author: codex

metadata.title: Round-trip import corrupts title + drops type ([Type] tag not parsed back)
metadata.description: The exporter (renderDefaultMarkdown) appends each open item's type as a trailing ' [Type]' annotation, but the importer (parseMarkdownTodos) only strips the '<!-- pm-id -->' provenance comment — never the [Type] tag. With the new --upsert (2026.6.5), re-import now MATCHES by pm-id and UPDATES the existing item, so the round-trip: (1) folds '[Type]' into the title, and (2) resets type to the global default (Task). The corruption COMPOUNDS: each export->import --upsert appends another tag ('Crash on startup' -> '[Issue]' -> '[Issue] [Task]'). Confirmed against published pm-todos@2026.6.5.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.acceptance_criteria: export -> import --upsert leaves title and type byte-identical; trailing [Type] tag parsed back into --type only when a pm-id provenance comment is present; hand-written lines ending in [foo] are untouched (default path stable); existing 52 tests still pass + new round-trip-fidelity tests added
metadata.resolution: Type tag parsed back into the item type, gated on a real pm-id provenance comment + the exact PM_ITEM_TYPES vocabulary; upsert-update disambiguates title-content brackets via the matched item's stored title (whitespace-normalized); never bulk-retypes tagless lines. Hardened across 6 review rounds.
metadata.expected_result: export -> import --upsert leaves title, type, and status byte-identical for open, closed, and checked-off items; hand-written/technical brackets ([WIP], [Safari]) untouched
metadata.actual_result: Verified end-to-end: lossless across N round-trips for all item states incl. titles ending in type-name brackets; 72 tests green; CI + CodeRabbit + gemini all clean
metadata.close_reason: Fixed in PR #6 (merged ae05e50): round-trip the exporter's [Type] tag without corrupting titles
